admission"}],"showConjunctionBetweenSections":false,"academicYearId":0,"institutionParentId":0,"instruction":{"type":"NFromArea","amount":1.0,"amountQuantifier":"None","amountUnitType":"Course","toAmountDeterminer":"None","toAmount":0.0,"areaType":"None","id":"bd8c1ef5-eb52-4859-9301-08ddb3499762","selectionType":"Complete"},"advisements":[],"groupId":"53b17645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":7,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

The Managerial Economics major at UC Davis is a disciplinary program combining strong preparation in microeconomic theory and quantitative methods. It prepares students for the analysis of management and policy issues in business, finance, marketing, production, agriculture, food distribution, natural resources, the environment, resource allocation, and international trade and development. Students in the Managerial Economics program develop valuable skills and strengths that lead to careers in business and government. Students specialize in one or more emphases selected from the following:


  • Business Economics focuses on the economic aspects of managerial decision-making essential for solving problems in business, management, marketing, and finance.


  • International Business Economics explores the economic drivers and policy challenges in the major emerging markets and focuses on how these markets are impacting the world economy.


  • Environmental & Resource Economics concentrates on issues related to the use of resources and environmental quality.


  • Agribusiness Economics focuses on the economic and policy aspects of the production and marketing of foods and fibers.

Internships for students in Managerial Economics provide additional career experience, information, and preparation in a variety of business enterprises and governmental agencies. For more information, see Internship & Career Center. Managerial Economics majors have established careers in every area of business and industry, including marketing, analysis, consulting, financial services, accounting, entrepreneurship, real estate and government. Many graduates have entered advanced degree programs in business management, accounting, finance, agricultural and resource economics, economics, public policy, and law.

","groupId":"f8de7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"ACCOUNTING","groupId":"6d0d7945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":9,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"BUSINESS LAW","groupId":"2b0e7945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":11,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Group 2: The MAT 016ABC option is acceptable if the student started prior to Fall, 2024 and must also include either PLS 021 or ECS 032A. Please see the Managerial Economics major in the catalog and the department articulation agreements in prior academic years.

","groupId":"ceb47645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":9,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

At the time of TAG submission, Managerial Economics requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.00 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.

","groupId":"e2b47645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":7,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.

 

Important Information:


Complete the courses below and maintain a 2.00 GPA across the entire group:


○    Economics (ECN) 001A/B

○    Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 019A/B/C or 021A/B/C

○    Statistics (STA) 013 or 032


Attention: MAT 16A/B/C accepted if taken before fall 2024


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

","groupId":"e5b47645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"General"}]
